$comment: D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY (plan 24 Stage 3, INCREMENT 3) end-to-end witness for the now-iterative postfix-CALL arm of the CST->HIR expression lowerer (`lowerExpr` in src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp). `id` is the identity function `int id(int x){return x;}`; the program returns `id(id(id(...id(42)...)))` — a 150-deep chain of single-argument calls (just under the parser's 256 maxExpressionDepth cap) whose innermost argument is `42`. Each call's sole argument is itself a call, so before inc-3 `lowerPostfix`'s Call arm recursed into `lowerExpr(arg)` -> `lowerPostfix` 150 host frames deep during CST->HIR lowering; now the Call frame on `lowerExpr`'s explicit work-stack carries that descent (callee built first, then each scalar argument lowered through the work-stack) with FLAT O(1) host-stack cost per level (only the heap `work` + `callCtxs` vectors grow). Because `id` returns its argument unchanged, the whole chain evaluates to the innermost `42` — the return value, which is miscompile-sensitive: a dropped/duplicated call, a callee/argument build mis-order, or a wrong argument coercion would flip the exit code. Before this stage the lowering ran on the 64 MiB worker stack (core/substrate/large_stack_call) and 150 levels was already past the host's ~1 MB main-stack crash point (~25 levels), so an accidental main-stack regression would crash (rc-127) here instead of exiting 42. OUTPUT-IDENTITY against the prior recursive lowerer is enforced by the full ctest suite (the multi-argument coerce/order path is covered by the existing call goldens); this example adds the deep-flatten + real-codegen run witness for the Call arm specifically (the companions deep_expr_lower / deep_ternary_chain cover paren/binary/unary and ternary). The optimizedPipelines arm proves the lowered HIR survives the full release pipeline (the baseline arm is the real-codegen witness; the calls to a non-inlined identity function remain a genuine call chain through the optimized pipeline). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
